name: Full Adder
description: A Full Adder adds three single-bit binary numbers (A, B, and Carry-in) and produces two outputs: Sum and Carry-out. Built using two Half Adders and an OR gate. Essential for building multi-bit adders in ALUs.

tutorial.title: Building a Full Adder from Half Adders

steps.instruction: A Full Adder extends the Half Adder by adding a third input: Carry-in (Cin). This allows chaining adders for multi-bit arithmetic.
steps.expected_action: Observe the three inputs: A, B, and Cin.

steps.instruction: The first Half Adder (XOR0 + AND0) processes inputs A and B, producing a partial sum and carry.
steps.expected_action: Toggle A and B to see the first Half Adder behavior.

steps.instruction: The second Half Adder (XOR1 + AND1) adds the partial sum from the first stage with Cin.
steps.expected_action: Toggle Cin to see how it affects the second stage.

steps.instruction: The OR gate combines carry outputs from both Half Adders to produce the final Carry-out.
steps.expected_action: Find input combinations that produce Cout=1.

steps.instruction: Verify the truth table: A+B+Cin produces Sum (XOR of all three) and Cout (1 if 2+ inputs are 1).
Excellent! Full Adders can be chained to create ripple-carry adders for any bit width. This is how CPUs perform addition.
steps.expected_action: You now understand the building block of arithmetic logic units!
